title: Method and system for playing musical instruments
abstract: The present invention relates to a system for playing a plucked string instrument, the system comprising a plectrum, for stroking strings, provided with two electrically separated electrodes and a body, one electrode being positioned at one side of the body, the other electrode being position at the opposite side of the body, the system further comprising a plucked string instrument wherein at least two strings are electrically insulated from each other. Either the electrodes of the plectrum are connected to a voltage source, while the at least two strings of the plucked string instrument are connected to an electrical detector, or the at least two strings of the plucked string instrument are connected to a voltage source while the electrodes of the plectrum are electrically connected to a voltage source. In both cases, the system can detect electrical contact between one of the two electrodes and one of the strings.
claims: Claims A system for playing a musical instrument, the system comprising a detection system for detecting sensorimotor preparation for physical interaction of a player with the musical instrument, a controller for receiving data signals representative for the sensorimotor preparation for physical interaction from the detection system and for predicting a physical interaction with the musical instrument prior to said physical interaction with the musical instrument based on the received data signals, said predicting being performed using reservoir computing. A system according to claim 1, wherein said predicting is performed using a recursive neural network. A system according to any of the previous claims, wherein said predicting a physical interaction comprises predicting any or a combination of an onset, a location of physical interaction with the musical instrument, an intensity of physical interaction with the musical instrument, an articulation variation or a timbral variation, pitch choices in melodic or harmonic playing and/or their intervallic relationship to other pitch choices, rhythmic note values in relation to the meter and tempo of the current musical context, data collection for statistical analysis and community interactions or educational purposes. A system according to any of the previous claims, wherein the system comprises furthermore a module for further audio processing based on the predicted physical interaction obtained using the controller. A system according to any of the previous claims, wherein the controller furthermore is configured for inducing an augmentation of the sound produced by the musical instrument based on the predicted physical interaction with the musical instrument. A system according to any of the previous claims, wherein the controller furthermore is configured for providing feedback to the player for training purposes, direction purposes or conducting purposes. A system according to any of the previous claims, wherein the detection system for detecting sensorimotor preparation for physical interaction with the musical instrument is a detection system for detecting movement of the player with respect to the musical instrument. 8. A system according to any of the previous claims, wherein the detection system comprises one or more of an optical camera or infrared camera, a radar, an ultrasone detector, a vibration sensor, a gyroscope, an accelerometer, a piezodetector or a hall sensor. 9. A system according to any of the previous claims, wherein the system furthermore is adapted for providing haptic feedback to the player. 10. A system according to any of the previous claims, wherein the musical instrument is any of a string based plucking instrument, a string instrument, a percussion instrument or a wind instrument. 11. A system according to any of the previous claims, the system comprising - a plectrum (100) for stroking strings, the plectrum being provided with two electrically separated electrodes (101, 102) and a plectrum body (103), one electrode being positioned at one side of the plectrum body (103), the other electrode being positioned at an opposite side of the plectrum body (103), and - a plucked string instrument (200) wherein at least 2 of the strings (202, 203, 204, 205) are made of electrically conductive material and wherein the at least 2 of the strings are electrically insulated from each other, wherein either the electrodes of the plectrum (100) or the at least 2 of the strings (202, 203, 204, 205) of the plucked string instrument are electrically connected to a voltage source (110, 111, 206, 207, 208, 209), and wherein the remaining of the electrodes of the plectrum (100) or the at least 2 of the strings (202, 203, 204, 205) are connected to an electrical detector (210) for detecting an electrical contact between one of the two electrodes (101, 102) and one of the at least 2 of the strings (202, 203, 204, 205), wherein furthermore the detection system for detecting sensorimotor preparation for physical interaction of a player with the musical instrument is integrated in the plectrum. 12. A system according to claim 11, wherein the detection system furthermore comprises a piezo sensor (400) and/or an accelerometer (410). 13. A system according to any of claims 11 to 12, wherein the controller is adapted for predicting whether a stroke on the string or strings will be an up-stroke or a down-stroke, and/or for deriving which string will be stroke. 14. A system according to any of the previous claims, wherein the controller is adapted for detecting ancillary gestures of the player indicative for control of a sound controlling system. 15. Use of a system according to any of claims 1 to 14 for sound manipulation. 16. Use of a system according to any of claims 1 to 14 for training purposes. 17. Use of a system according to any of claims 11 to 14 for prediction of a stroke on a plucked string instrument.
